2603.16428.title: An Efficient Heterogeneous Co-Design for Fine-Tuning on a Single GPU
2603.16428.paper_summary: Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) has become essential for domain adaptation, but its memory-intensive property exceeds the capabilities of most GPUs. To address this challenge and democratize LLM fine-tuning, we present SlideFormer, a novel system designed for single-GPU environments. Our innovations are: (1) A lightweight asynchronous engine that treats the GPU as a sliding window and overlaps GPU computation with CPU updates and multi-tier I/O. (2) A highly efficient heterogeneous memory management scheme significantly reduces peak memory usage. (3) Optimized Triton kernels to solve key bottlenecks and integrated advanced I/O. This collaborative design enables fine-tuning of the latest 123B+ models on a single RTX 4090, supporting up to 8x larger batch sizes and 6x larger models. In evaluations, SlideFormer achieves 1.40x to 6.27x higher throughput while roughly halving CPU/GPU memory usage compared to baselines, sustaining >95% peak performance on both NVIDIA and AMD GPUs.
2603.16428.paper_summary_zh: 微調大型語言模型（LLMs）已成為領域適應的必要條件，但其內存密集型特性超出了大多數GPU的能力。為了解決這一挑戰並使LLM微調民主化，我們提出了SlideFormer，這是一個為單GPU環境設計的新系統。我們的創新包括：（1）一個輕量級的異步引擎，將GPU視為滑動窗口，並將GPU計算與CPU更新和多層I/O重疊。（2）一個高效的異構內存管理方案顯著減少了峰值內存使用量。（3）優化的Triton內核以解決關鍵瓶頸並集成先進的I/O。這種協作設計使得在單個RTX 4090上微調最新的123B+模型成為可能，支持高達8倍的批量大小和6倍的模型。在評估中，SlideFormer的吞吐量比基準高出1.40倍至6.27倍，同時將CPU/GPU內存使用量大約減半，在NVIDIA和AMD GPU上保持超過95%的峰值性能。
2603.16428.author: Ruijia Yang et.al.
